business_meaning: Direct proxy for demand-supply gap. Longer wait = fewer available drivers = higher surge.
Cyclical hour encoding (sin component).
Festivals (Diwali/Holi/Eid) spike demand 1.8x. Unique to Indian market.
Binary festival flag. Strong categorical demand signal.
Festival intensity (Diwali=3 > Eid=2 > Regular=0). Granular demand signal.
Raw hour captures intra-day demand curve (office peaks at 8AM, 7PM).
Longer rides: drivers more willing → slightly lower surge at high distance.
Cyclical: ensures model knows 11PM and 1AM are 2 hours apart, not 22.
City-level fare tier. Higher-fare cities have different driver density.
Encoded weather category — captures ordinal weather patterns.
Mumbai and Delhi have structurally higher base demand than Ahmedabad.
Raw day — Monday commute differs from Friday evening patterns.
Cyclical day-of-week (cos component).
1–5 severity scale. Heavy Rain=5 reduces supply while increasing demand.
Whether ride was accepted — strong proxy for price tolerance at current surge.
Cyclical day-of-week (sin): Monday and Sunday are 1 day apart.
8AM–10AM office commute. Second busiest window of the day.
Encoded segment: affects cancellation probability and acceptance rate.
Budget customers cancel at 1.5x. Premium tolerate 3x. Changes cancellation rate.
Interaction feature: rain DURING peak hours compounds surge multiplicatively.
Heavy rain specifically — strongest single weather signal.
10PM–2AM: driver supply drops sharply. Surge nearly guaranteed.
6PM–9PM office return + dinner = highest daily demand window.
People abandon autos/bikes → Uber demand spikes instantly.
Weekends shift demand from office to leisure — different surge profile.
Sat/Sun 7PM–10PM: combines leisure surge + late-night supply drop.
